text: The laparoscopic view shows the repair of a Morgagni hernia in a 32-year-old male. The surgeon uses grasping forceps to pull down the herniated greater omentum from the anterior diaphragmatic defect and places it into the abdominal cavity. The hernia sac is dissected and excised from the thoracic region.
The surgeon removes the excised hernia sac using an endobag, revealing the dimensions of the diaphragmatic defect in the thoracic cavity.

The surgeon repairs the diaphragmatic defect using interrupted non-absorbable sutures, including X-shaped and U-shaped mattress stitches. A tailored composite polypropylene mesh is applied over the defect and secured with absorbable fixation devices.

No drains were placed after the procedure. The patient made an eventful recovery and was discharged on the fifth postoperative day. He was symptom free at five years follow up. The control CT scan showed no hernia recurrence.
